book.title: Built to Last
book.author: Jim Collins and Jerry I. Porras

chapters.title: Build the Clock, Not Just One Great Moment

summaryBullets: Building institutions that keep producing results instead of relying on one brilliant leader or product creates clearer judgment.
Great organizations outlast personalities when the system is stronger than the star.
This matters because durability depends on what keeps working after the obvious hero leaves.
A common mistake is confusing one strong era with an enduring company.
The pattern becomes easier to see when performance depends on one figure, one product, or one lucky cycle.
A practical starting move is to build systems that outlast the founder, design for succession and continuity, and strengthen the operating principles.
keyTakeaways: Building institutions that keep producing results instead of relying on one brilliant leader or product creates clearer judgment. This perspective helps people make clearer decisions and follow through with more discipline when stakes rise.
practice: build systems that outlast the founder
design for succession and continuity
strengthen the operating principles
test whether the organization still works without one hero

chapters.title: Preserve the Core and Stimulate Progress

summaryBullets: Holding a stable core while still pushing for adaptation and change creates clearer judgment.
Strong organizations pair identity with experimentation instead of choosing one.
This matters because durability improves when values stay clear but methods keep evolving.
A common mistake is treating consistency as an excuse to resist needed change.
The pattern becomes easier to see when the culture sounds principled but the organization cannot adapt with the environment.
A practical starting move is to name the non negotiable core, separate values from current tactics, and experiment around the core.
keyTakeaways: Holding a stable core while still pushing for adaptation and change creates clearer judgment. This perspective helps people make clearer decisions and follow through with more discipline when stakes rise.
practice: name the non negotiable core
separate values from current tactics
experiment around the core
review whether progress is still real
